phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Introduction to food science foundations, chemistry, and career pathways in food safety, quality, and product development.

milestones.label: Explore food science career pathways
milestones.description: Survey of food science principles including food chemistry, processing, preservation, safety, and quality. Students explore career pathways in FDA, USDA, food manufacturing, and quality assurance through industry guest speakers and facility visits.

milestones.label: Build chemistry foundations
milestones.courseRef: CHEM 106/116 — General Chemistry I with Lab
milestones.description: Foundational chemistry including atomic structure, bonding, stoichiometry, and solutions. Laboratory component develops analytical skills essential for food chemistry and quality testing.

milestones.label: Study biological science fundamentals
milestones.description: Cell biology, genetics, and microbial diversity fundamentals. Provides the biological basis for understanding food microbiology, fermentation, and bioprocessing.

milestones.label: Complete quantitative reasoning
milestones.description: Mathematical foundations for statistical analysis, process engineering calculations, and quality control metrics used throughout the food science curriculum.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Intermediate food chemistry, organic chemistry, and microbiology build the scientific core for IFT competency standards.

milestones.label: Master food chemistry principles
milestones.description: Chemical properties of major food components — water, carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and enzymes. Students analyze chemical reactions during processing and storage using spectrophotometry, chromatography, and wet chemistry methods.

milestones.label: Study food safety & HACCP systems
milestones.description: Food safety management systems including HACCP, FDA regulations, sanitation standard operating procedures, and global food safety standards (GFSI, ISO 22000). Students develop HACCP plans for real food products.

milestones.label: Understand food processing & engineering
milestones.description: Principles of thermal processing, dehydration, refrigeration, and novel food processing technologies. Students apply mass and energy balance calculations to food manufacturing unit operations.

milestones.label: Complete food microbiology foundations
milestones.description: Microbial diversity, pathogenesis, and food-borne illness prevention. Laboratory training in culture methods, identification protocols, and environmental monitoring relevant to food manufacturing environments.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Advanced food microbiology, product development, and sensory evaluation build professional-grade expertise aligned with IFT standards.

milestones.label: Master food microbiology & quality
milestones.description: Advanced study of microorganisms in food systems including spoilage, fermentation, and pathogen control. Students conduct microbiological testing using industry-standard methods (BAM, AOAC) and develop environmental monitoring programs.

milestones.label: Develop food products from concept to market
milestones.description: Complete product development cycle from concept ideation through prototype, consumer testing, and commercialization planning. Teams create novel food products addressing real market opportunities and present to industry panels.

milestones.label: Conduct sensory evaluation research
milestones.description: Scientific methods for evaluating food sensory properties including discrimination, descriptive, and affective testing. Students design and execute sensory panels, analyze data statistically, and interpret consumer preference patterns.

milestones.label: Master food analysis instrumentation
milestones.description: Advanced instrumental analysis of food composition including proximate analysis, chromatographic separation (HPLC, GC), spectroscopic methods, and physical property measurement. Builds competency for quality control laboratory management.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Capstone project, industry internship, and advanced specialization complete IFT competency standards and career preparation.

milestones.label: Complete food science capstone project
milestones.description: Independent or team-based research project addressing a real food industry challenge. Students design experiments, analyze data, and present findings to faculty and industry professionals in a conference-style format.

milestones.label: Complete professional internship
milestones.description: Supervised internship at a food manufacturing company, regulatory agency, or research institution. Students apply IFT competencies in authentic industry settings and build professional networks for career placement.

milestones.label: Master advanced food science elective
milestones.description: Specialized study in areas such as food biotechnology, global food systems, nutraceuticals, or food regulatory affairs. Students deepen expertise in their chosen career focus area.

milestones.label: Build cross-disciplinary competency
milestones.description: Elective in business, marketing, chemistry, or a related field that broadens professional perspective and supports career goals in food industry management, entrepreneurship, or graduate study.
